title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The UK government has made a response to a report on Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards, outlining their position and policies on AI in the public sector. The response aims to address the recommendations made by the Committee in 2020.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This report looks at how copyright laws affect the development of artificial intelligence systems, especially when they use copyrighted material. It likely assesses the potential impact of AI systems using copyrighted works on copyright holders and the public.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education has a policy on generative AI in education, outlining its stance on the use of this technology. The policy aims to guide schools and institutions in making informed decisions about the integration of generative AI into educational environments.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: This action plan outlines a strategy for the Department for Transport (DfT) to collaborate with the transport sector and utilize Artificial Intelligence (AI) technology, balancing benefits and potential risks associated with its adoption. The plan aims to harness AI's advantages while mitigating its disadvantages.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This report covers the use of copyrighted materials in the development of artificial intelligence systems. It provides an update on the progress made under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act regarding AI and copyright works.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government is responding to recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council to regulate Artificial Intelligence used as a medical device. This response aims to ensure AI medical devices are safe and effective for use.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: This policy document outlines Ofqual's approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in qualifications. It aims to set a framework for the responsible use of AI in the qualifications sector, responding to government guidance.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: This document outlines the UK government's response to the House of Lords Select Committee's report 'AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency'. The government sets out its plans to address the committee's recommendations on artificial intelligence in the UK.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This document outlines the terms of reference and frequently asked questions for a committee reviewing the standards of artificial intelligence and its impact on the public. It aims to establish guidelines and provide clarity on AI and public standards.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The Civil Service Policy Legislation (CSPL) has provided written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee on how to govern Artificial Intelligence. This submission aims to inform the development of policies that regulate AI.
